requirement_content: The medical diagnosis system must be formalized using an encoder-decoder architecture framework
The system must include separate encoders for patient history (text), lab results (structured data), and medical imaging (images)
The system must support real-time processing to provide quick diagnostic suggestions during patient consultations
The system must provide explainable diagnostic decisions with attention mechanisms highlighting influential input data
The system must use a unified encoder that processes all input modalities together using transformer-based architecture with modality-specific embeddings
The encoder architecture must process multi-modal inputs (patient history, lab results, imaging) with separate modality-specific encoders
The decoder architecture must generate diagnostic predictions and probabilities from fused multi-modal representations
The system must support real-time processing with explainable diagnostic decisions, combining low-latency inference with attention-based explanations for clinical usability
The system must comply with HIPAA regulations and ensure patient data encryption, anonymization, and audit logging
The system must support real-time processing with comprehensive explainability including both attention-based explanations and counterfactual reasoning showing alternative diagnostic scenarios
